name: Confluence
description: Atlassian's cloud wiki and knowledge management platform; yields space permission exports, site and organisation audit logs, public link (external sharing) configuration and page history evidence of policy review cadence.

steps.action: Open your Confluence site, select the settings wheel in the top-right corner to enter Confluence administration, then under Monitoring select Audit log and set the date range for the reporting period
steps.audit_note: The site audit log is not available on the Free plan; events are retained for a configurable 1-12 months (default one year), so capture regularly before the retention window closes

steps.action: Export the filtered audit log to CSV from the same screen and save it with the capture date
steps.audit_note: The site audit log records space, permission, public link and admin events but explicitly excludes content edits — pair it with page history evidence for content changes

steps.action: For a site-wide permissions report, go to Confluence administration, under Admin tools select Export permissions data, then select Export all permissions combinations and start the export; for individual critical spaces, open the space, select More actions (•••) > Space settings and screenshot the Space permissions page
steps.permissions_required: Site admin (permissions export); Space Admin (per-space screenshots)
steps.audit_note: The site-wide export is only available on sites that have not yet fully moved to Confluence role-based access; on fully migrated sites rely on per-space permission screenshots instead

steps.action: For external sharing evidence, go to Confluence administration > Settings > Security, open the Public links tab and screenshot the site-level 'Allow public links on this site' toggle plus the per-space allowed/not allowed list
steps.audit_note: This page is the authoritative record of your external sharing posture; on Premium and Enterprise plans it also shows spaces blocked from allowing public links

steps.action: For policy review cadence evidence, open each in-scope policy page, select More actions (•••) > Page history and screenshot the version list (version number, author, date); optionally insert the Change history macro on a policy register page for an always-current view
steps.permissions_required: View permission on the policy pages
steps.audit_note: Page versions are timestamped by Confluence and retained indefinitely, making version history a reliable record that policies were reviewed on schedule

steps.action: Upload the CSV exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Ensure no API tokens or restricted page content is visible in screenshots, and restrict access to exports containing user email addresses

L2.title: Atlassian Organisation Audit Log Exports
L2.estimated_time: 30 minutes initial setup, then 10 minutes per export

steps.action: Confirm the Confluence site sits under your Atlassian organisation at admin.atlassian.com — the organisation audit log records user and admin management activity across products, and an Atlassian Guard subscription extends its coverage and retention
Navigate to admin.atlassian.com > Security > Audit log, filter by product (Confluence) and set the date range for the reporting period
Select Export to download a CSV of the filtered events (up to the 10,000 most recent events per export)
steps.audit_note: The organisation audit log retains activity for a limited window (around 180 days with Atlassian Guard); exporting monthly preserves evidence before the retention window closes

steps.action: In Confluence administration, set the site audit log retention to the maximum 12 months, then set a recurring monthly task for both exports — Confluence has no natively scheduled evidence delivery — and record each run against the evidence ID
steps.audit_note: Setting maximum retention gives you a recovery buffer if a monthly export run is missed

steps.action: File each CSV in the evidence repository with restricted access
steps.security_note: Exports contain user email addresses and IP addresses; store them in a location with access limited to the compliance team

steps.action: Create credentials: set up a dedicated service account with Confluence Administrator global permission, then create an API token with an expiry at id.atlassian.com > Security > API tokens while signed in as that account
steps.permissions_required: Confluence Administrator (the authenticating account); Site admin to grant the global permission
steps.security_note: The token is shown only once at creation — store it in a secrets manager, and use the service account rather than a personal admin login

steps.action: Pull the site audit log daily: GET https://your-domain.atlassian.net/wiki/rest/api/audit/since?number=1&units=days for incremental records, or GET /wiki/rest/api/audit/export for a CSV/ZIP export, authenticating with Basic auth (account email + API token)
steps.audit_note: Daily exports preserve audit records beyond the configurable 1-12 month retention period; OAuth apps need the read:audit-log:confluence scope

steps.action: Run a daily space permission audit: GET https://your-domain.atlassian.net/wiki/api/v2/spaces to enumerate spaces, then GET /wiki/api/v2/spaces/{id}/permissions for each in-scope space, following cursor pagination (limit up to 250 per page)
steps.permissions_required: View permission on each space (use the Confluence admin service account for full coverage)
steps.audit_note: Capturing permission assignments daily gives you point-in-time evidence for access reviews without relying on the deprecating site-wide export

steps.action: Capture policy review cadence programmatically: GET https://your-domain.atlassian.net/wiki/api/v2/pages/{page-id}/versions for each in-scope policy page and store the version list (number, author, created date)
steps.permissions_required: View permission on the policy pages

steps.action: Collect organisation audit events programmatically: create an organisation API key in admin.atlassian.com > Settings > API keys, then call GET https://api.atlassian.com/admin/v1/orgs/{orgId}/events with Bearer authentication and from/to/cursor parameters
steps.permissions_required: Organisation Admin (to create the API key)
steps.security_note: Organisation API keys are highly privileged; scope usage to read operations, store the key securely and rotate it regularly
steps.audit_note: This endpoint is rate-limited to around 10 requests per minute; batch daily pulls rather than polling aggressively

steps.action: Add error handling and validation: follow pagination cursors and 'next' links to completion, alert on authentication failures or empty result windows, and store outputs with run timestamps and checksums
steps.audit_note: Recording run metadata and checksums makes each collected file independently verifiable

steps.action: Stream audit events continuously: with a Confluence Cloud Enterprise plan or Atlassian Guard Premium, go to admin.atlassian.com > Security > Audit log > Settings > Webhook tab and register up to three webhooks that deliver organisation audit log activity to your SIEM or collector in near real time
steps.security_note: Configure the optional authorisation credentials on the webhook and validate them at the receiving endpoint so forged events cannot enter the evidence store
steps.audit_note: Audit log webhooks are plan-gated (Enterprise plan or Guard Premium); confirm entitlement before designing the pipeline around them

steps.action: Without those plans, fall back to continuous cursor-based polling of GET https://api.atlassian.com/admin/v1/orgs/{orgId}/events combined with daily GET /wiki/rest/api/audit/export pulls from the site audit log
steps.permissions_required: Organisation Admin (API key holder); Confluence Administrator (site audit export)

steps.action: Run a daily drift job that compares space permissions (GET /wiki/api/v2/spaces/{id}/permissions) and the public links posture against your approved baseline, alerting on newly public-link-enabled spaces, anonymous access grants or unexpected space admins
Reconcile daily: compare webhook-received event counts against the organisation events API and the site audit export for the same window, and alert on gaps or webhook delivery failures
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics from reconciliation are what turn continuous collection into audit-grade evidence

steps.action: Publish a compliance dashboard: spaces allowing public links, space permission review coverage, and policy pages overdue for review (derived from page version history), reported weekly
Quarterly, rotate the service account API token and organisation API keys, and review the Confluence Administrator and Organisation Admin lists
steps.security_note: Expired or orphaned automation credentials are a common finding; tie rotation to the quarterly access review
